meanings.definition: move out of or depart from
examples: the fugitive has left the country

meanings.definition: take out of a container or enclosed space
examples: Get out your best dress--we are going to a party!
The troops pulled out after the cease-fire
I managed to get out a few words

meanings.definition: bring, take, or pull out of a container or from under a cover
examples: The mugger pulled a knife on his victim

meanings.definition: be released or become known; of news
examples: News of her death broke in the morning

meanings.definition: escape potentially unpleasant consequences; get away with a forbidden action
examples: I couldn't get out from under these responsibilities
